text_excerpt: Latest News | Democratic Unionist Party | DUP Branding News Our Team Policies About Us Contact Donate Join Icon Sign In News Our Team Policies About Us Contact Donate Join Icon Sign In Icon Live News Feed 13 May, 2026 McIlveen welcomes £500,000 veterans boost Strangford DUP MLA Michelle McIlveen has welcomed news that veterans’ charity Beyond the Battlefield has secured significant funding to provide accommodation and support for homeless former members of the Armed Forces. 13 May, 2026 Robinson reacts to King’s Speech 12 May, 2026 DUP delivering on childcare while SF actions will add cost - Brooks DUP MLA David Brooks has hit out at Sinn Fein hypocrisy on childcare costs following comments made in the Assembly by Sinn Fein MLA Cathy Mason. 11 May, 2026 Delays in justice system a “shocking indictment” – Cameron 11 May, 2026 Tourism NI should promote Northern Ireland with confidence DUP North Belfast MLA Phillip Brett has criticised Tourism NI after the body repeatedly referred to Northern Ireland as “the north” in official communications. 11 May, 2026 Backing Belfast City Airport Master Plan will deliver jobs and growth – DUP DUP MLAs Phillip Brett, Diane Forsythe and Jonathan Buckley have tabled a motion in the Assembly supporting the George Best Belfast City Airport Master Plan 2040. 09 May, 2026 Robinson comments on election results across GB DUP Leader Gavin Robinson MP said: 08 May, 2026 New Richhill Office Strengthens Local DUP Presence - Robinson DUP Leader Gavin Robinson MP has officially opened Gareth Wilson MLA’s new constituency office in Richhill, describing it as a positive step in strengthening representation and support for local communities across Newry & Armagh. 08 May, 2026 Supreme Court ruling must now be acted upon – Robinson DUP Leader Gavin Robinson MP has said the Equality Commission must now issue clear guidance to public bodies across Northern Ireland following the latest Supreme Court ruling. 07 May, 2026 SF blaming wrong Minister on Place Name Project - Bradley DUP MLA Maurice Bradley has criticised Sinn Fein Communities Committee Chair Colm Gildernew for what he described as a failure to grasp the basic facts surrounding the Northern Ireland Place Name Project. 1 2 3 4 5 ... 102 Top Stories McIlveen welcomes £500,000 veterans boost 13 May, 2026 DUP delivering on childcare while SF actions will add cost -
